# Hash-Provenance Note — `rf-gro-002` fixture bundle

**Task**: P1-T6 (Multi-Bundle Conversion E1, Phase 1 — Rights-Aware Vendoring & Fixtures)
**Source `rf` run**: `RF-GRO-002` — `rf_run_20260717_rf_gro_002_pediatric_cds_evidence`
(intent `intent_research_20260717_rf_gro_002_pediatric_cds_evidence`; local mirror read from
`research-foundry/runs/rf_run_20260717_rf_gro_002_pediatric_cds_evidence/`; the run's own
`reviews/verification.yaml` records `passed: true`, `exit_code: 0`;
`docs/project_plans/expansion/rf-handoff/RESULTS.md` §1 independently confirms it `verified`).
**Selection rationale**: per this plan's FR-2 (row P1-T6), `RF-GRO-002` is one of the 4 clinical
bundles (alongside `RF-EV-001`, `RF-CBC-002`, `RF-KID-001`) this plan converts into fixtures —
it is not legal-review-flagged the way `REG-001`/`REG-004` are. It carries 12 source cards / 92
claims (79 supported, 10 inference, 3 speculation, 0 mixed/contradicted/unsupported) — matching
`rf-handoff/RESULTS.md`'s row for `RF-GRO-002` exactly.
**Generated by**: `node scripts/evidence/generate-rf-fixture.mjs --run-dir <RF-GRO-002 run dir>
--slug gro-002` (P1-T1's bundle-parametrized generator); regenerating with `--check` against the
committed tree reports all 30 generated files byte-identical (zero drift).
**Derived**: 2026-07-22.

## 1. Content-rights disposition (applies to every passage in this bundle)

Every one of the 12 source cards in `RF-GRO-002` carries the same `usage` block:

```
usage: {allowed_for_public_output: false, allowed_for_work_output: true,
        allowed_for_personal_meatywiki: true, citation_required: true,
        quote_limit_notes: "Short excerpts only."}
```

**No passage in this run is positively confirmed rights-clear for committing verbatim text to
this repository.** Per this plan's decisions block and ADR-0002's rights-restricted fallback
(applied structurally by `generate-rf-fixture.mjs`, not by hand), this fixture therefore defaults
**every single passage** to the restricted disposition: the record retains an immutable
**SHA-256 hash** of the original verbatim excerpt plus its **precise selector** (the sibling
`locator` field and the source card's own `evidence_id`), but never the exact article text
itself. This is a uniform disposition, not a per-passage judgment call: 12/12 source cards →
79/79 extracted passages → **rights disposition: restricted (hash + selector only)**, 0/79
positively confirmed rights-clear.

| # | Source card | Title | Publisher | Evidence points (passages) | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 00 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_00` | Use of World Health Organization and CDC Growth Charts for Children Aged 0-59 Months in the United States | CDC / MMWR Recommendations and Reports | 6 | restricted |
| 01 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_01` | WHO Child Growth Standards: Weight-for-length/height | World Health Organization (WHO) | 5 | restricted |
| 02 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_02` | WHO Child Growth Standards: Length/height-for-age | World Health Organization | 7 | restricted |
| 03 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_03` | Risk Factors for Postnatal Growth Faltering and Undernutrition at Discharge in Very Preterm Infants: A Retrospective Study Applying the ESPGHAN Consensus Definitions | Nutrients (MDPI) | 7 | restricted |
| 04 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_04` | Creation and Evaluation of New Growth Charts With a Gradual Transition From WHO to CDC Values | American Academy of Pediatrics (Pediatrics) | 6 | restricted |
| 05 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_05` | An Update on the Diagnosis and Management of Faltering Growth and Catch-Up Growth in Young Children | S. Karger AG (Annals of Nutrition and Metabolism) | 8 | restricted |
| 06 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_06` | Development of a WHO growth reference for school-aged children and adolescents | Bulletin of the World Health Organization | 6 | restricted |
| 07 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_07` | Consensus statement of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics/American Society for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition: indicators recommended for the identification and documentation of pediatric malnutrition (undernutrition) | Nutrition in Clinical Practice (ASPEN / Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics) | 6 | restricted |
| 08 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_08` | Risk Assessment of Cognitive Impairment at 2 Years of Age in Infants Born Extremely Preterm Using the INTERGROWTH-21st Growth Standards | The Journal of Pediatrics (Elsevier); NICHD Neonatal Research Network | 8 | restricted |
| 09 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_09` | Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children (NICE guideline NG75) | National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) | 8 | restricted |
| 10 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_10` | Growth Faltering and Failure to Thrive in Children | American Academy of Family Physicians (American Family Physician) | 6 | restricted |
| 11 | `src_20260718_rfgro002_11` | Consensus Statement of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics/American Society for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition: Indicators Recommended for the Identification and Documentation of Pediatric Malnutrition (Undernutrition) | American Society for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition (A.S.P.E.N.); Nutrition in Clinical Practice (SAGE) | 6 | restricted |
| | | | **Total** | **79** | **79/79 restricted** |

Note the WHO-vs-CDC growth-standard conflict this bundle documents (cards 00, 04, 06 vs. cards
01/02) is preserved as-is at the source-card level in this fixture; resolving it into an explicit
named conflict-visible object is P5-T2's job (`modules/growth_suite_v1/` projection), out of
scope for this fixture-vendoring task.

## 2. What was sanitized, and how

Only the **verbatim exact-passage excerpts** were redacted — everything else in the bundle
(claim ledger, extraction cards, report, verification record, RF's own paraphrased summaries,
population/assay/lifecycle metadata, source metadata, locators) is copied byte-for-byte
unmodified, because `generate-rf-fixture.mjs`'s `assertNeverCarriesExcerptField` mechanically
confirmed none of it reproduces the underlying articles' text at length (generation would have
failed closed, naming the offending file/line, had any of these artifact kinds carried an
unexpected `quote:`/`passage_locator:` construct):

- `sources/src_*.md` — each source card's `extracted_points[].quote` field (the YAML
  frontmatter verbatim excerpt) and the matching `— quote: "..."` line in the markdown
  "Key evidence" section were replaced with
  `[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:<hash>]`,
  where `<hash>` is the SHA-256 of the original excerpt's exact bytes (both occurrences of the
  same excerpt hash identically, so a reviewer can confirm the YAML and markdown copies agree
  without ever seeing the text). The embedded excerpt inside each non-null
  `threshold.passage_locator` flow-map value (which mixed a human-readable selector prefix with a
  second copy of the verbatim excerpt) was likewise replaced by its own SHA-256 hash — the
  human-readable selector is preserved undiminished in the sibling `locator` field one level up,
  so no addressability is lost. 17 of the 79 `passage_locator` values are `null` (no excerpt to
  redact) and were left untouched.
- `extractions/ext_*.yaml` — copied unmodified; the generator's `assertNeverCarriesExcerptField`
  confirmed all 12 files carry only `text` (RF's own paraphrase) and `locator` (selector), never
  a `quote`/verbatim-excerpt field.
- `claims/claim_ledger.yaml`, `reports/report_draft.md`, `reviews/verification.yaml`,
  `research_brief.md`, `swarm_plan.yaml`, `evidence_bundle.yaml`,
  `writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml` — copied unmodified; the same mechanical check confirmed no
  verbatim source-article excerpts (claim text and report sentences are RF's own
  paraphrased/synthesized prose with `[claim:clm_id]` references, not reproduced article text).

Total redactions: **219** (157 `quote` fields — 79 frontmatter `extracted_points[].quote` values
+ 78 mirrored markdown-body "Key evidence" bullet quotes, one card's bullets fold two adjacent
`extracted_points` into a single mirrored line — + 62 non-null `passage_locator` embedded
excerpts). The full per-redaction ledger (file, line, field, excerpt length, SHA-256) is
machine-readable JSON at
[`passage-hash-ledger.json.txt`](./passage-hash-ledger.json.txt) in this directory (named with a
`.json.txt`, not a bare `.json`, extension — see §2.1, same reason as `rf-cbc-001`'s fixture).

### 2.1 Why the ledger isn't named `*.json`

Same coupling `rf-cbc-001/HASH-PROVENANCE.md` §2.1 documents:
`scripts/evidence/backfill-rule-governance.mjs` computes each rule's `requiredTestCaseIds` by
recursively walking `tests/witness/` **and `tests/fixtures/`** for every `*.json` file and
running it through the engine as if it were a patient-intake fixture. A non-intake `.json` blob
dropped anywhere under `tests/fixtures/` derives to an almost-entirely-missing fact set, which the
KB's generic missing-data safety rule would treat as a witness and append to its
`requiredTestCaseIds` — silently drifting the **committed** `modules/anemia/rules.json`. Since
`modules/anemia/` is read-only for this task, the ledger is named `passage-hash-ledger.json.txt`
(content is still plain JSON — parse it with `JSON.parse()` as normal) so the coverage scan's
`endsWith('.json')` filter skips it. Every other file in this fixture keeps its native `rf`
extension (`.yaml`/`.md`, matching the run's own source-card/extraction-card/bundle formats) —
none of those are `.json` either, so none of them touch this coupling.

## 3. Bundle hash (fixture as committed)

SHA-256 computed over the sorted `relative_path:sha256(bytes)` pairs of every artifact file in
this fixture directory (i.e. the mirrored `RF-GRO-002` bundle: `evidence_bundle.yaml`,
`research_brief.md`, `swarm_plan.yaml`, `claims/claim_ledger.yaml`, `reports/report_draft.md`,
`reviews/verification.yaml`, `writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml`, all 12 `sources/src_*.md`, all 12
`extractions/ext_*.yaml` — 31 files total; this note and `passage-hash-ledger.json.txt` are
excluded from the bundle hash as fixture-derivation metadata, not `rf` bundle content):

```
bundleSha256: sha256:f99ded41fe4926425489dee6c44851ca6d82f399b72cabed1f08d3b284189ffd
fileCount: 31
```

Per-file SHA-256 (original run file → fixture file) is recorded in
`passage-hash-ledger.json.txt`'s `files[]` array for every copied artifact, alongside the
per-passage redaction hashes. Regenerating in-memory via `node scripts/evidence/generate-rf-fixture.mjs
--run-dir <RF-GRO-002 run dir> --slug gro-002 --check` reports all 30 generator-managed files
(everything but `HASH-PROVENANCE.md` and `passage-hash-ledger.json.txt`, which the generator
deliberately does not write) as matching the committed tree byte-for-byte.

## 4. Directory layout (mirrors `evidence_bundle.yaml.artifacts`)

```
tests/fixtures/rf-gro-002/
  evidence_bundle.yaml          (copied unmodified)
  research_brief.md             (copied unmodified)
  swarm_plan.yaml                (copied unmodified)
  claims/claim_ledger.yaml       (copied unmodified)
  reports/report_draft.md        (copied unmodified)
  reviews/verification.yaml      (copied unmodified)
  writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml    (copied unmodified)
  sources/src_20260718_rfgro002_{00..11}.md   (sanitized — see §2)
  extractions/ext_20260718_*.yaml (12 files, copied unmodified)
  passage-hash-ledger.json.txt   (this fixture's own derivation metadata, not `rf` content —
                                   `.json.txt`, not `.json`; see §2.1 for why)
  HASH-PROVENANCE.md             (this note)
```

Paths are resolved exactly as `evidence_bundle.yaml.artifacts` declares them
(`source_cards_dir: sources/`, `extraction_cards_dir: extractions/`, etc.) so `tools/rf-bundle-to-kb-pack/`'s
read-only bundle loader can resolve every artifact without any hard-coded assumption. Note this
run's `reports/` directory also carries a `report_deterministic.md` in the live `rf` run — that
file is not declared in `evidence_bundle.yaml.artifacts` and is therefore not part of the mirrored
fixture tree, consistent with the generator only ever copying artifacts the bundle manifest
declares.

## 4.1 Current `inspect` status (honesty note, not a fixture defect)

`node tools/rf-bundle-to-kb-pack/cli.mjs inspect --run-dir tests/fixtures/rf-gro-002 --module
modules/growth_suite_v1/module.json` currently exits non-zero with `DecisionsNotFoundError:
authoring-decisions.yaml not found at .../modules/growth_suite_v1/authoring-decisions.yaml`. This
is expected and is **not** a defect in this fixture: `growth_suite_v1` is a brand-new greenfield
module scaffold (P3-T2) that — per FR-9/FR-22 — deliberately has no authoring-decisions record
yet in this pass (authoring decisions remain an explicit human clinical-authorship act, `02
§4.12`, out of scope here). The same `DecisionsNotFoundError` reproduces identically against
`tests/fixtures/rf-ev-001` (`modules/anemia/module.json`) and `tests/fixtures/rf-kid-001`
(`modules/kidney_suite_v1/module.json`) — it is a property of the target module lacking a
decisions file, not of any one fixture. Only `tests/fixtures/rf-cbc-002` currently inspects clean,
because `modules/cbc_suite_v1/` already carries an `authoring-decisions.yaml` from prior work.
This fixture's own file inventory, determinism (`--check` reports all 30 generator-managed files
byte-identical), and rights disposition are independently verified in §§1-3 above regardless of
this module-side gap; resolving `inspect --run-dir tests/fixtures/rf-gro-002 ...` to a clean exit
is tracked at P1-GATE / later authoring-decisions work, not this task.

## 5. Non-goals of this note

This note does not reopen any of the parent plan's open questions or decisions block. It
documents only how this one fixture bundle was derived from `RF-GRO-002` and what was redacted
and why — a rights/hash record, not a clinical-content or rule-authoring decision.
